app.title: EXIFmod: Image Metadata Editor for Analog Workflows

updater.updateAvailableDetail: EXIFmod {{version}} is available. Download the update now?
updater.downloadedDetail: The update has been downloaded. Restart EXIFmod to install it.
updater.devOnlyDetail: Automatic updates are available only in the signed release app.

tutorial.firstRunLead: Here is a quick tour of the basics.
tutorial.step1Body: EXIFmod helps you apply consistent metadata (camera, lens, film, author, exposure, description, and keywords) across photos using a saved preset catalog, then writes those values into your image files with ExifTool. Edits stay pending until you commit them.
tutorial.step2Body: Start with <blue>{{openFolder}}</blue>. The app lists supported images in that folder (JPEG and TIFF). Select one or more files to load their current metadata and preview.
tutorial.step3Body: Apply presets and other metadata to selected files using the <green>Metadata</green> pane. If a local Ollama server is available, the <icon></icon> AI button can suggest <green>Description</green> and <green>Keywords</green>. All of your changes will be staged as <pending>{{pendingBadge}}</pending>.
tutorial.step4Body: To write your pending changes, click <red>{{writePending}}</red>. To preview the EXIF tags that will be written to each file, expand <green>{{previewExif}}</green>.
tutorial.step5Body: Use <gear></gear> to create and edit presets.

dialog.exportChooseFolder: Choose a folder. The preset database will be saved as presets.sqlite3.
dialog.replaceSqliteDetail:
A file named presets.sqlite3 already exists in:
{{dir}}
dialog.ollamaSpawnFailed: Could not start Ollama. Make sure it is installed.
dialog.ollamaStillUnreachable: Ollama did not start in time.
dialog.installLrPluginNotMacDetail: Installing the Lightroom Classic plug-in is only supported on macOS.
dialog.installLrPluginMissingDetail: EXIFmod could not find the bundled {{path}}. Try reinstalling EXIFmod, or install the plug-in manually from the application repository (extras/lightroom-classic-exifmod-open).

importExport.importDetail:
Imported {{count}} preset(s) from previously exported database:
{{path}}
importExport.exportInvalid:
Cannot export: preset database is invalid:
{{detail}}

preflight.exiftoolNotFound:
exiftool not found.
Install exiftool and ensure it is available in PATH or one of the common install locations.

mergeImport.invalidDb:
The selected file is not a valid EXIFmod preset database:

{{detail}}
mergeImport.skipDuplicate: A preset named "{{name}}" already exists in category "{{category}}".

ipc.finderMultiPathDetail: Open only one file or folder at a time.

preview.decodeFailed: Preview could not be decoded. For TIFF and other raw formats, ensure the file is readable.
preview.tooLarge: Image is too large to preview without decoding.

ui.errorPreloadBody: EXIFmod must run in the desktop app window (e.g. start with npm run dev), not in a standalone browser tab—the connection to file handling and metadata is unavailable. If you already launch Electron and still see this, run npx electron-vite build once so out/preload/index.mjs is built.
ui.removeMixed: Mixed: selected files differ for remove
ui.notesPlaceholderMixed: Selected images have different descriptions. Text you type here becomes the same pending description for every selected file, replacing their differing values. The AI control appends to the pending New Value for each file.
ui.keywordsPlaceholderMixed: Selected images have different keywords. Keywords you type here become the same pending keywords for every selected file, replacing their differing values. The AI control appends suggested keywords to each file’s pending New Value and dedupes.
ui.copyCurrentToNew: Copy Current Value to New Value: {{row}}
ui.aiBatchConfirmLead: Generate AI Descriptions and Keywords for {{count}} File(s)?
ui.aiBatchConfirmDetail: For each file, AI output appends to the pending New Description (within the EXIF byte limit), and suggested keywords append to pending New Keywords with dedupe. Film-identifying keywords already present in the file are preserved.
ui.aiBatchErrorsLead: Could Not Complete AI for {{failed}} of {{total}} File(s).
ui.aiBatchErrorsDetail: Review the messages below. You can close this dialog or retry only the files that failed.
ui.aiDescribeTooltip: Generate description and keywords with AI (Ollama)
ui.aiDescribeOllamaUnavailable: AI (Ollama) is not available this session.
ui.aiDescribeOllamaNotInstalled: Install Ollama to use AI for generating descriptions and keywords.
ui.aiDescribeOllamaWaitingStart: Use Start Ollama next to this button to enable AI.
ui.aiDescribeNoRoom: There is no space left for new text in Description (EXIF ImageDescription is limited to 999 UTF-8 bytes, including a separator when appending). Remove some text, then try again.
ui.aiDescribeNoRoomTooltip: No room left in Description (999 UTF-8 byte limit).
ui.ollamaEmptySoftFailure: The model returned no description or keywords. Try again.
ui.writeConfirmDetail: This will modify the original files.
ui.writeConfirmLrcDetail: At least one file has Lightroom / Camera Raw develop metadata embedded in the image (not a sidecar). In Lightroom Classic, use Metadata → Save Metadata to File before editing here if you need the catalog and disk in sync. After writing, avoid Metadata → Read Metadata from File unless you intend to replace catalog metadata from disk.
ui.writeConfirmLrcSnapshot: If you plan to use Read Metadata from File after writing here, consider creating a Develop Snapshot in Lightroom Classic first (Develop module → Snapshots → +), so you can restore your develop settings afterward. One recovery flow: snapshot → write in EXIFmod → Read Metadata from File → re-apply the snapshot.
ui.lrcSnapshotModalBody: Before you write metadata from EXIFmod, consider creating a Develop Snapshot in Lightroom Classic (Develop module → Snapshots → +). That gives you a restore point if you later use Metadata → Read Metadata from File and need to get your develop settings back.
ui.lrcSnapshotModalMetadataHint: If the catalog and disk must stay in sync, use Metadata → Save Metadata to File in Lightroom Classic before editing here.
ui.lrcSnapshotModalDontShowAgain: Do not show this again when opening from Lightroom Classic
ui.clearConfirmLead: Discard All Uncommitted Metadata Edits for Files in This Folder?
ui.quitPendingDetail: You have pending edits that are not written to your image files. Quitting now discards those edits.
ui.resizeListPreview: Resize file list and image preview

statusFooter.applicationCheckingBody: Verifying ExifTool, the preset catalog, and the desktop connection.
statusFooter.applicationOkBody: ExifTool and the preset catalog are available for this session.
statusFooter.ollamaReadyBody: Ollama is reachable. Use the AI button in Description and keywords when files are selected.
statusFooter.updaterIdleBody: No update activity. Choose Help → Check for Updates to look for a new release.

presetEditor.name: Preset Name
presetEditor.copyrightWrittenNone: No Copyright tag will be written.
presetEditor.fixedShutterValueRequired: Enter a shutter speed or turn off fixed shutter speed.
presetEditor.fixedApertureValueRequired: Enter an aperture or turn off fixed aperture.

validation.exposureTime: Shutter speed: use a fraction (e.g. 1/125) or decimal seconds (e.g. 2).
validation.fNumber: Aperture: enter a positive f-number (e.g. 2.8 or 8).
